title: Aleuritia kingii
taxonomy: Aleuritia kingii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is a member of the genus Aleuritia, which is a part of the Rubiaceae family. Aleuritia kingii is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of the plant is further divided into several taxonomic ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Aleuritia kingii is closely related to other flowering plants in the same family.

intro: Aleuritia kingii, also known as King's Gentian, is a plant species in the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its unique, delicate appearance and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as respiratory issues and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Aleuritia kingii include gentiopicroside, loganin, and swertiamarin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aleuritia kingii is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture to leverage its health benefits.
